faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Click on the question to see or hide the answer.

banner.text2: for an overview of Rivers.run features!

q1.q: How does the app work offline?
q1.p1: For the best experience, download the native app from the App Store or Google Play. The app (and the website itself!) is built "offline-first". This means it automatically downloads map tiles, river coordinates, and flow data in the background.
q1.p2: Simply open the app or website while you still have an internet connection (like on the drive to the put-in). It will silently fetch the latest flow limits so you can view accurately colored rivers later on when you lose cell service!

q2.q: What do the different colors on the rivers mean?
q2.p1: We use a standardized color scale based on community-sourced threshold data to instantly show river runnability:
q2.lowDesc: Scrappy or low water, but navigable.
q2.tooHighDesc: Flood levels or generally unsafe/blown out.

q3.q: How do I add a new river?
q3.p2: button in the navigation menu (or the [+] icon). Fill out the form with the river's basic details and click Submit. Our administrators will review your suggestion before it's published to the global map.

q4.q: A river's flow limits or access markers are wrong. How do I fix it?
q4.p1: Rivers.run relies on crowdsourced data! Click on any river in the list to expand its details window, then click the
q4.p3: Make your changes and submit them. They will go directly into the admin queue for review.

q5.q: I found a bug or need administrative help. Who do I contact?
q5.p2: . This is our only monitored support inbox for technical features or administrative requests.

q6.q: I would like to help build rivers.run
q6.p1: Assistance is always welcome! Please direct your inquiries to
q6.p2: . Please be clear that Rivers.run is independently developed and compensation will not be offered for contributions.
